News & Updates
Moss will present an abstract of her research, Petrologic and Geophysical Synthesis of the Magmatic System at Akutan Volcano, at the American Geophysical Union Annual Meeting on 12/18
Moss will present with Dr. Xiaotao Yang on Akutan Volcano at the SZ4D-supported workshop “Research Prioritization in the Alaska-Aleutian Arc” on 12/14

title: CV
text:
Maggie Moss 
maggie.moss@colostate.edu | Fort Collins, CO | 570-624-6960 
 
Professional Summary 
Volcanology, Geochemistry, & Petrology PhD Candidate with four years of experience in academic research, writing, and data analysis. Strong research background in petrological and geochemical disciplines, as well as ample experience in planning and conducting fieldwork in volcanic environments. Aspiring to leverage skills to conduct petrological and geochemical research on the Yellowstone Plateau Volcanic Field basaltic plumbing system. 
 
Education 
Ph.D. Geosciences, Colorado State University, Fort Collins, CO, expected December 2029  

GPA: 4.09 | McCallum Mineralogy & Petrology Scholar 



Supervisor: Dr. Lauren Harrison 



Dissertation: Igneous petrology, geochemistry and geochronology of basaltic plumbing systems through time at the Yellowstone Plateau Volcanic Field 

 
Master of Urban Planning, NYU Wagner School of Public Service, NYC, NY, 2023 

GPA: 3.87 | Global Gallatin Fellowship in Urban Practice 



Capstone Project: The Impact of SFHA Designation on Neighborhood Development in NYC Post Hurricane Sandy 

 
Bachelor of Science, Geological Sciences, George Washington University, Washington, D.C., 2019 

GPA: 3.60 | Dean’s List, Departmental Honors, Magna Cum Laude 



Undergraduate Research Thesis: Analysis of Distal Lava Creek Tuff at Yellowstone Supervolcano: Insights into the thermal state of large-volume silicic magmas and the cooling history of related deposits 




Honors & Awards 


Jack Kleinman Memorial Grant for Volcano Research 


Colorado Scientific Society Research Grant 


McCallum Mineralogy & Petrology Scholarship 


Joby Adams Geosciences Graduate Scholarship 


Gallatin Global Fellowship in Urban Practice 


Fulbright Research Scholar Semi-Finalist 


Luther Rice Undergraduate Research Scholarship 


KACIF Research Grant 


Mineralogical Society of D.C. Research Award  



Publications 

Field Guide to the Cascades Magmatic Arc (Forthcoming textbook in partnership with Dr. Richard Tollo with interest from Elsevier publishing) 



Green, Healthy Schools: A Blueprint for Mayor Adams (Climate Works for All, 2022) 



Local Hiring for Green Infrastructure Development: One Path Toward Environmental & Economic Justice in NYC (ALIGN NY, Whitepaper, 2021) 

Abstracts & Presentations 
Moss, M., Myers, M., Mark, H., Yang, X., Cheng, Y., Gapenthin, R., Lizik, Y., Portner, D., Lee, D., Wu, S., Larsen, J., Coombs, M., Petrologic and Geophysical Synthesis of the Magmatic System at Akutan Volcano. AGU Fall Meeting, New Orleans, LA, December 2025. (First Author, Presenting) 
 
The Impact of SFHA Designation on Neighborhood Development in New York City Post Hurricane Sandy (NYU 2022) 
 
Research  
 
Research Associate, Denver Museum of Nature & Science | May 2025 - Present 
Intern, Denver Museum of Nature & Science | May 2025 - Present 
Conducting geologic research on the Rocky Mountains, Colorado Plateau, and Western U.S. basin and range geologic settings; creating paleomaps of the western US for use in forthcoming museum exhibits.  
 
Research Assistant, Dr. Lauren Harrison, CSU Geosciences | Jul 2025 - Present 
Conducting field, geochronology, and geochemical research on Junction Butte Basalt and Undine Falls Basalt, oldest basaltic units of the Yellowstone Plateau Volcanic Field. 
 
Data Analytics Fellow, Urban Democracy Lab | Dec 2020 - Oct 2021 
Conducted research in support of the Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act, resulting in publication, Green, Healthy Schools: A Blueprint for Mayor Adams (Climate Works for All, 2022). 
 
Research Assistant, Dr. Richard Tollo, GWU Geology | 2017-2019 
Conducted research at Mt. Rogers, Yellowstone National Park, and the Cascades Magmatic Arc, resulting in forthcoming publication, Field Guide to the Cascades Magmatic Arc (Tollo).  
 
Teaching Experience 
Graduate Teaching Assistant 
Colorado State University | January 2025 – May 2025 | Fort Collins, CO 

In collaboration with faculty, creates laboratory curriculum for undergraduate geology courses, gives weekly laboratory lectures in support of main lecture topics, and grades lab assignments and final exams.  



Igneous & Metamorphic Petrology (GEOL 364)  

 
Graduate Teaching Assistant 
New York University | Aug 2020 – May 2021 & Jan 2022 - May 2022 | New York, NY  

Taught statistical and data analytics laboratory course to NYU graduate students on topics such as housing finance, zoning, and transportation.  



Supported NYU Wagner faculty with curriculum research, online content creation for events, and other administrative projects to enhance the school’s profile and provide support to admitted students.  

 
Undergraduate Teaching Assistant 
George Washington University | Aug 2017 - Dec 2018 | Washington, D.C. 

In collaboration with tenured faculty, created laboratory curriculum for undergraduate introductory geology courses, gave weekly laboratory lectures in support of main lecture topics, and graded lab assignments and final exams.  



Served as laboratory learning support for undergraduate students in Mineralogy and Igneous & Metamorphic Petrology courses, and assisted professors with grading lab assignments. 

 
Volunteer Work 
Stablehand (Horse Caretaker) 
Charis Youth Ranch, Fort Collins, CO | Aug 2024 – Present  
O4U Advisory Board Member (2023-2024) 
 
Skills 
ArcGIS | R | Stata | MatLab | Adobe Illustrator | Microsoft Suite | Chinese Language 
 
Workshops/Short Courses 
Diffusion Chronometry | RUBION, Ruhr-University | Bochum, Germany | October 2025 
Alaska Aleutian Arc Workshop | Alaska Volcano Observatory | Anchorage, AK | May 2025  
Exoplanets by the Lake II | University of Munich | Munich, Germany | July 2024
